flow: CVA6 supported regression flow (verif/sim/cva6.py); per-test tandem run of the Verilator RTL model (veri-testharness) and the Spike golden ISS, with RVFI trace-CSV comparison. Same evidence class as core-v-verif.

model.isolation: Verilated into a private symlink-farm tree (build/isa-conformance/cva6-iso) with its own work-ver, so the shared external/cva6/cva6/work-ver model held by the benchmark agent was never built into or clobbered.

skipped_tests.reason: upstream testlist iterations:0 (tdata1 read differs spike 0x2000000000000000 vs cva6 0x0)
upstream testlist iterations:0 (cva6 does not record illegal instructions in trace log)
upstream testlist iterations:0 (error with spike reference)

suites.name: riscv-arch-test
suites.description: RISC-V architectural compliance suite (riscv-non-isa/riscv-arch-test), Spike as reference signature model
suites.blocked_reason: Host assembler incompatibility, not an RTL/ISS defect. The xPack GNU binutils 15.2.0 assembler rejects the TEST_JALR_OP macro expansion in riscv-test-suite/rv64i_m/I/src/jalr-01.S line 158: "Error: illegal operands `la x0,5b'" (newer binutils disallows the pseudo-instruction `la` targeting x0/zero). cva6.py treats a gcc compile failure as fatal and aborts the run; 15 tests passed before the abort. Reproduced deterministically and load-independently.
suites.remediation: Build/use the riscv-arch-test reference toolchain expected by ARCH_TEST_HASH a5a49fc9 (an older riscv64-unknown-elf binutils that accepts `la x0,...`), or patch the affected riscv-arch-test sources, then re-run: bash scripts/run_e1_isa_conformance.sh riscv-arch-test

suites.name: riscv-compliance
suites.blocked_reason: Not run. The riscv-compliance testlist (testlist_riscv-compliance-cv64a6_imafdc_sv39.yaml) is vendored, but the legacy compliance flow shares the same riscv-arch-test source tree and toolchain, so it inherits the same xPack-binutils `la x0` assembler incompatibility blocking riscv-arch-test.
suites.remediation: Resolve the riscv-arch-test toolchain blocker above, then add a riscv-compliance branch to scripts/run_e1_isa_conformance.sh using verif/regress/dv-riscv-compliance.sh as the reference.

verdict: The cv64a6_imafdc_sv39 RTL model (== E1 e1-pro little core) passes 224/224 executed CVA6 riscv-tests directed ISA tests (118 machine-mode + 106 Sv39 virtual-mode), 0 failures, each verified by RVFI trace comparison against the Spike golden ISS. The 6 testlist entries that did not execute are upstream CVA6 iterations:0 exclusions (documented spike-vs-cva6 M-mode trap/CSR trace divergences), not E1 defects. This is measured ISA-correctness evidence of the same class core-v-verif provides, valid at L1_RTL_FULL_SOC. riscv-arch-test and riscv-compliance are fail-closed BLOCKED on a host-toolchain assembler incompatibility (xPack binutils 15.2.0 vs the older binutils the suite expects), not on the model.
claim_ladder_note: Per docs/benchmarks/claim-ladder.md L1: functional correctness (workload runs to completion, outputs match a golden reference) is supportable; this evidence supports ISA functional correctness only. It does NOT support wall-clock/phone-class performance, silicon power, or Android/CTS claims.
